calendar.matters: Petition For Surcharge Of Former Administrator
REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION

Petition For Surcharge Of Former Administrator
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jennifer Ordonez, Esq. appearing representing Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company, objector.
Sheila K. Robello, Esq. appearing representing Michon Coleman, Petitioner/Successor Administrator Coleman.
George McNitt, Esq., Administrator of the Estate of Frederick Coleman appearing.
Stephanie Gonzalez, Esq. appearing representing Naima Jahi-Coleman, Objector.
Phillip Campbell, Esq. appearing as an interested party that use to represent the deceased.

Petition to approve settlement between Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company, objector and Petitioner Michon Coleman to be filed. Petition for Surcharge of Former Administrator is continued to Jul-15-2026 at 09:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Continued To Jun-29-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-20-2026 Continued To May-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Surcharge Of Former Administrator
Off Calendar Apr-06-2026 Continued To Jun-01-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sheila Robello for successor administrator Michon Coleman (via Zoom).
Jennifer Ordonez for objector Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company (via Zoom).
Stephanie Gonzalez for objector Naima Jahi-Coleman (via Zoom).
George McNitt, administrator of the Estate of Frederick Coleman (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Surcharge of Former Administrator.

The Court inquired regarding the status of a previously referenced settlement agreement. Ms. Robello reported that a draft settlement had been prepared and was acceptable to her client, but final approval from the surety remained pending. Ms. Ordonez confirmed that the matter was still under review by the surety and estimated a response within one to two weeks.

Mr. McNitt stated that a creditor's claim had been filed by the surety in the Estate of Frederick Coleman and requested to be included in communications regarding the settlement and as a signatory to any agreement. Ms. Robello stated that she would include the Estate of Frederick Coleman in the settlement.

The Court continued the matter to June 1, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Continued To Apr-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Surcharge Of Former Administrator
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Apr-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sheila Robello for petitioner Michon Coleman (via Zoom).
Ciera Teets for objector Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company (via Zoom).
George McNitt, interested party (via Zoom).
Stephanie Gonzalez for objector Naima Jahi-Coleman (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Surcharge of Former Administrator and for Status of Mediation.

The Court inquired regarding the status of mediation. Ms. Robello reported that mediation had occurred but did not result in a global resolution. She stated that a tentative agreement had been reached between the estate and one party and that a revised draft would be circulated in an effort to reach a global resolution with the remaining parties. Ms. Robello further noted that if a global resolution could not be reached, the estate intended to proceed with the partial agreement.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to April 6, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Surcharge Of Former Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Continued To Feb-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vincent Yu appeared via zoom for Michon Coleman (petitioner).
Mark Aronson appeared via zoom Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company (objector).
Daniel Newbold appeared via zoom for Naima Jahi-Coleman (objector).
George McNitt appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the surcharge, that the former administrator made early distributions, that bank records were omitted, and that the calculations are inaccurate and additional calculations should be provided.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 13, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 18, 2026. The matter is continued to February 23. 2026 and the Parties are to return on February 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The Court shall also order a Breslin order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cece. Not reported.

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-29-2025 Continued To Dec-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sheila Robello appeared via zoom for Michon Coleman (petitioner).
Rosa-Martinez-Genzon appeared via zoom Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company (objector).
George McNitt appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the surcharge petition, that Mr. Coleman failed to timely file state and federal income taxes, and that Ms. Robello will be filing a written response. Both matter are continued to December 1, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Newbold to file objection by October 10, 2025. Ms, Robello to file her reply by October 15, 2025.

Judge: Josh Fredricks. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phillip Campbell, Esq. specially appearing on behalf of Frederick Coleman, Petitioner (appearing by video conference).

At the outset, the Court notes that the matter before it is to admit to probate the lost Will of the Decedent, dated August 5, 1988 and appoint Frederick Coleman the administrator of the estate with Will Annexed.

After review of the pleadings and facts as stated in the Petition, the Court finds that the presumptions of the Probate Code pertaining to a lost Wills do not apply in the instant matter. The Court additionally finds that notice of the proceedings appears to be complete and that no objection has been made to the Petition. The Court grants the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The copy of the lost Will dated August 5, 1988 is admitted to probate. Frederick Coleman is appointed the Administrator of the estate with full authority under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Bond is set at $1,100,000.00. Frank Mahoney is appointed as Probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of the estate is set for hearing on October 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Singh. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
